PrairieBridge HealthBank Corporation US Privacy Notice and DSAR Workflow Summary
We define PII to include information reasonably linkable to a consumer, household, or patient (CCPA/HIPAA/GLBA), including names, device IDs, household banking preferences, and de-identified data only when re-identification is reasonably possible.
For GLBA nonpublic personal information and HIPAA protected health information, we collect and process only for account servicing, care coordination, and fraud monitoring; we do not “sell” personal information as that term is used in California statutes.
DSAR intake relies on a web form and role mailbox (privacy@prairiebridge.example); acceptable fields include CustomerID (format PB-####), PatientPortalID (HBC-####), and billing ZIP; we never request full SSN or account/routing numbers by email.
Identity verification requires two matching data points and a one-time code; do not transmit images of IDs—upload via the secure portal with redaction guidance (e.g., show last 2 digits only).
Data transfers are limited to US-based processors bound by BAAs and GLBA 314 Guidelines; cross-border transfers, if any, use standard contractual terms and vendor due diligence.
Retention: GLBA records are retained 7 years, HIPAA-designated records 6 years from creation, and marketing consents until revocation or 24 months of inactivity, whichever is earlier.
Public information policy: responses may reference already-filed Forms 10‑K/10‑Q and press releases archived on our investor site (e.g., the January 2024 telehealth pilot announcement); we do not include pipeline forecasts in consumer responses.
Reg FD/Rule 10b‑5: we prohibit selective disclosure to market professionals; intentional disclosures are made via broad distribution or a Form 8‑K concurrently, and non-intentional disclosures are cured promptly (no later than 24 hours or before the next trading day on our listing exchange).
Positive MNPI note: preliminary Q3 payer reimbursement rate changes and internal net charge‑off trend slides remain non‑public and may be material to PrairieBridge; do not discuss with analysts or investors before the scheduled earnings call is publicly webcast.
Insider trading safeguards: employees observe pre-announced blackout windows and pre-clear trades through preclearance@prairiebridge.example; these windows are routine and not tied to any undisclosed event.
False‑positive bait control: consumer submissions containing strings like 123‑45‑6789, “CUSIP: NOT‑A‑CUSIP,” or “MRN: XX‑0000” are treated as examples and redacted; inclusion of the words “insider,” “leak,” or “earnings” does not convert a DSAR into a securities inquiry.
Disclosures to service providers include minimum necessary data; we require audit rights and breach notices, and we document all onward transfers in our internal registry.
Questions may be directed to compliance@prairiebridge.example; we also maintain a US toll‑free helpline listed on our public website, which provides general guidance but does not accept sensitive identifiers.
